features.summary: NVIDIA reported Q1 fiscal 2024 revenue of $7.19 billion, a 19% sequential increase but a 13% decline year-on-year. The growth was primarily driven by record data center revenue of $4.28 billion, up 18% sequentially and 14% year-on-year, fueled by demand for accelerated computing and generative AI applications. Gaming revenue increased 22% sequentially to $2.24 billion, while professional visualization and automotive revenues showed mixed results.
features.management_outlook: For Q2 fiscal 2024, NVIDIA expects total revenue to reach approximately $11 billion, driven by strong demand in the data center segment related to generative AI. The company anticipates gross margins to improve to 68.6% GAAP and 70% non-GAAP, with significant supply chain procurement planned for the second half of the year to meet ongoing demand.
features.risks: NVIDIA faces risks related to supply chain constraints, particularly in meeting the surging demand for data center products driven by generative AI. Additionally, the competitive landscape is intensifying with new entrants and existing semiconductor companies vying for market share in accelerated computing and AI solutions.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of NVIDIA's growth include the increasing adoption of generative AI across various sectors, strong demand from cloud service providers, and the successful launch of new products such as the H100 GPUs and DGX Cloud services. The company's focus on providing a full-stack solution for accelerated computing positions it well to capitalize on the ongoing transition in data centers from traditional CPU-based systems to accelerated computing architectures.
